article_title: TypeScript 6.0 Beta Ships as the Last JavaScript-Based Release Before the Go Rewrite
summary: Rejected: This is a duplicate of an article already published on 2026-02-15 covering the same TypeScript 6.0 Beta story. This is the third TypeScript 6.0 Beta submission (PR #132 was also rejected for the same reason on 2026-03-01).

findings.details: An article covering the identical story was published on 2026-02-15: 'TypeScript 6.0 Beta Marks the End of an Era as Microsoft Prepares Go-Based Compiler That Builds 10x Faster' (slug: 2026-02/15-typescript-60-beta-marks-the-end-of-an-era-as-microsoft-prepares-go-based-compiler-that-builds-10x-faster). Both articles cover the same Microsoft TypeScript 6.0 Beta announcement from February 11, 2026, draw from the same primary source (devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0-beta/), report the same benchmark figures (VS Code 10.2x, Sentry 8.2x, TypeORM 9.9x), cover the same default changes (strict, esnext, es2025, empty types array), and describe the same deprecations (es5, --outFile, AMD/UMD/SystemJS, baseUrl).

findings.message: Third submission on same topic — PR #132 also rejected
findings.details: A previous submission on the same TypeScript 6.0 Beta topic was reviewed via PR #132 and rejected on 2026-03-01 with the explicit verdict REJECT citing duplicate content. The current submission (PR #143) was created the same day (2026-03-02) and covers the same news event with no new angle, no new developments, and no additional sources that were not already available at the time of the original publication.

content_preview.title: TypeScript 6.0 Beta Ships as the Last JavaScript-Based Release Before the Go Rewrite
content_preview.summary: Microsoft's TypeScript 6.0 beta marks the end of the original JavaScript codebase, clearing the path for a native Go rewrite in TypeScript 7.0 that promises 10x faster compilation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft released the beta of TypeScript 6.0 on February 11, 2026, describing it as a "transition release" and confirming it will be the last major version of the language built on the current JavaScript codebase. The milestone clears the runway for TypeScript 7.0, a full native rewrite in Go internally codenamed "Project Corsa," which has already demonstrated compilation speedups of up to 10x in real-world codebases.

As stated in the [official TypeScript blog announcement](https:...

recommendations: Do not resubmit this story. TypeScript 6.0 Beta is already covered in the published archive.
Before writing future articles, check src/content/articles/ to avoid duplicate coverage.

A follow-up article would be appropriate when TypeScript 6.0 reaches stable release (currently scheduled for 2026-03-17) or when TypeScript 7.0 ships in GA, covering only the new developments at that time.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written, technically detailed, and well-structured. The article itself is of good quality — the rejection is entirely due to duplication, not content failures.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified as reputable HTTPS sources from devblogs.microsoft.com and infoq.com. Source integrity passes.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Claims align with cited sources. Benchmark figures (VS Code 10.2x, Sentry 8.2x, TypeORM 9.9x) match the December 2025 progress update. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No AI self-reference, no sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: CRITICAL FAILURE: This article is a direct duplicate of content published on 2026-02-15 covering the exact same news event (Microsoft TypeScript 6.0 Beta announcement of February 11, 2026). Both articles use the same primary source, cite the same benchmark numbers, and describe the same breaking changes and deprecations. Furthermore, PR #132 (submitted 2026-03-01) was already rejected for the same reason — making this the third attempt to publish a duplicate TypeScript 6.0 Beta article. The bot has been explicitly instructed in the PR #132 review not to resubmit this story.
concerns: Third submission on identical topic despite explicit rejection of PR #132 the previous day for duplicate content.

The same primary source (devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0-beta/) and the same news event (February 11, 2026 beta announcement) underlie both the published article and this submission.
No new angle, no new developments, no updated benchmarks, and no editorial differentiation from the already-published article.
recommendations: Do not resubmit this story in its current form.
Implement a pre-submission check against the published articles archive to prevent duplicate submissions.
When TypeScript 6.0 reaches stable release or TypeScript 7.0 ships, a genuinely new article covering only the new developments would be appropriate and welcome.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Rejection required. Despite passing all technical and integrity checks, this submission duplicates an article already published on 2026-02-15. The bot was explicitly told in the PR #132 review not to resubmit this story. Publishing this would result in redundant content and undermine the credibility of the editorial process.
